dialogos.texto_en: Oh, so this is where the rest area is?\nWe ended up coming all the way back to the entrance.
This place is quite spacious, isn't it?\nI wonder what's over there?
Phew...\nI could use a cold drink.
(I think I'll take a break for a bit.)
(*sigh* I'm finally free...)
Oh, there's a sampling corner over here!
These are 'sata andagi' doughnuts.\nI think I'll have one too.
(They seem to be obsessed with the dessert souvenirs,\n maybe this will calm them down a bit.)
Hey, Nino.\nDo you know how to make these?
I don't know...\nbut it's like a fried doughnut, right?\nI don't think it's that difficult.
Do you want to try making it yourself?
Yeah.\nIf I can make it,\nit would be a good snack for everyone.
Agreed!\nWe can also enjoy the Okinawa vibe at home!
If this was a little less sweet,\nit might go well with tea or even black tea, right?
On the contrary, coating it with chocolate\nmight also be good, right?
I'll pass on the chocolate...
Let's consider the flavors that each of us like.
(Geez...\n No matter where we go, they're always so noisy.)
(Well, thanks to that,\n we don't get bored being together.)
Uesugi-kun, can I talk to you for a moment?
Itsuki?\nWhat's wrong, what's going on?
(For her to come here without trying the samples...\n it must be a really important matter.)
You said I could speak freely,\nso I'll take you up on that offer.
Regarding what we were talking about earlier?\nThere must be something going on after all.
Yes.\nThere is something I absolutely must ask you.
...What do you think will happen to the relationship \nbetween all of us from now on?
I'm not really sure how to say this.\nWe each have our own paths, \nso I don't think it will be the same as before.
Indeed, there is no doubt that things will change.
Thanks to you,\nwe were able to safely graduate high school.
Yeah, it was really tough.
I think no matter how much we express our gratitude,\nit won't be enough.
And, with that%p-2;――
The tutor-student relationship between us\nhas come to a complete end, hasn't it?
...The condition was to get you guys to graduate.
There's no reason for us to be together anymore.
Although we're friends,\nonce you go to Tokyo, there is no guarantee\nthat we can maintain the same relationship as before.
When we decided on this trip,\nyou said it was for 'making one last memory,' right?
...Yeah, I did.
Is that 'last' supposed to assume \nwe're going to part ways?
I didn't mean it that way.
Just as you said, from now on,\nthe time we spend together as the six of us\nwill lessen, right?
That's why I really wanted to cherish this trip.
...We don't know when the next one will be.
It might be the last time, after all...
That's a possibility.\nI plan to go back home in the summer,\nso we might all be together again then.
...So, next summer?
Do you intend to make us wait again until then?
You're going to mess with our feelings by keeping \nus hanging without ever giving an answer, aren't you?
That's...
If you look at how Nino and Miku have been acting,\neven you can understand, right?
I'm sure you can sense Ichika's determination too.\nAnd...
Even so, are you really planning to leave\nwithout saying anything to us?
(As I thought, these guys\n want me to give them an answer.)
(...This is a problem that can't be resolved\n unless I do it myself.)
*sigh*
As expected, you're still you even at times like this.
Huh?\nWhat? That was abrupt.
(Even though you're always clumsy and ill-mannered, \n Itsuki, you really...hit the nail on the head at \n just the right moment.)
Nah, it's nothing.
Itsuki.\nI don't intend to just abandon this problem.
...Then what kind of answer do you plan to give?
I still don't know.
Even at this point, you still...!
Hey.\nDo you need a reason to be with someone?
Huh?
Didn't you say it earlier?\n'There's no reason to be together anymore.'
That's...
You guys are sisters and family.\nI understand that's the reason for being together.
But I'm different.\nSomething like a reason%p-2;――
I think it's just because I want to be with them.\nThat's all.
Is that enough for you?
I'm sure it is. But with you five...\nthere's just too much going on,\nand it's all too confusing.
Regrettably, I agree with that.\nHonestly, I'm not sure either...
(All I need now is confidence\n and determination.)
